title: [EPIC 0] Foundation
body:
## Epic goal
Lay the substrate before any page work. Branch, design tokens, MDX pipeline, CI guards. Blocks every other epic.

## Definition of done
- Design context files (`.claude/` + `CLAUDE.md`) committed at repo root
- `redesign` branch deployed to Vercel preview
- Design tokens (palette, typography, spacing) live in `app/globals.css`
- MDX content pipeline working for `_data/projects`, `_data/essays`, `_data/experience`, `_data/credentials`
- CI passing: TypeScript strict, Biome/ESLint, Lighthouse mobile ≥ 90 on PR previews
- All sub-issues closed: #0.0–#0.6

## Sub-issues
- [ ] [0.0] Add design context files + CLAUDE.md to repo root
- [ ] [0.1] Set up redesign branch + feature-flag routing
- [ ] [0.2] Install + configure design tokens (palette + typography)
- [ ] [0.3] Build shared layout primitives
- [ ] [0.4] Set up MDX + content pipeline
- [ ] [0.5] Set up CI guards
- [ ] [0.6] Audit + migrate content sources (backend API → MDX, if applicable)

title: [0.0] Add design context files + CLAUDE.md to repo root
body:
## Goal
Place the design system documentation and Claude Code context files at the repo root so every Claude Code session starts with the right context.

## Context
This is the unlock for AI-assisted development on this project. Once these files exist, every Claude Code session inherits the design language, voice, and engineering conventions without re-explaining them. **Do this first — before any code work.**

## Scope — in
- Create `CLAUDE.md` at repo root (entry point that Claude Code reads first)
- Create `.claude/` directory with:
  - `README.md` — entry guide
  - `design-system.md` — palette, typography, spacing, layout grammar
  - `editorial-voice.md` — anti-AI manifesto, voice samples
  - `component-patterns.md` — reusable component patterns
  - `mockups/` — the six HTML mockup files
    - `homepage.html` (from homepage-v2-pink.html)
    - `work.html` (from work-mockup.html)
    - `writing.html` (from writing-mockup.html)
    - `experience.html` (from experience-mockup.html)
    - `credentials.html` (from credentials-mockup.html)
    - `skills.html` (from skills-mockup.html)

## Scope — out
- Do NOT add any code, only documentation/reference files
- Do NOT modify any existing source files

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `CLAUDE.md` exists at repo root and is committed to `main`
- [ ] `.claude/` directory exists with all 4 markdown files
- [ ] All 6 mockup HTML files in `.claude/mockups/` (renamed to short names)
- [ ] Opening `.claude/mockups/homepage.html` in a browser renders correctly
- [ ] All files committed to `main` (these are reference files, not branch-specific)

## Reference materials
- Files provided in the setup ZIP / Gist (link in PR description)

title: [0.1] Set up redesign branch + feature-flag routing
body:
## Goal
Create the `redesign` branch and configure `/v2/*` routing so the new design ships next to the live design without breaking production.

## Context
The redesign is a side-by-side refactor, not a rebuild. The current site stays live while v2 is built piece-by-piece. Blocks every other issue in Epic 0 and beyond.

## Scope — in
- Create `redesign` branch from `main`
- Create `app/(v2)/` route group (or `app/v2/` if using Pages Router conventions — confirm App Router)
- Add basic `app/(v2)/layout.tsx` placeholder
- Add `app/(v2)/page.tsx` placeholder with "V2 coming soon"
- Verify both `/` (current) and `/v2` (new placeholder) render on Vercel preview

## Scope — out
- Do NOT modify existing routes
- Do NOT add design tokens yet (issue #0.2)
- Do NOT add MDX pipeline yet (issue #0.4)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `redesign` branch exists and is pushed to remote
- [ ] Vercel preview URL renders `/v2` with placeholder content
- [ ] Existing `/` route renders unchanged
- [ ] No TypeScript errors
- [ ] Branch protection rule: PRs to `redesign` require passing CI

## Reference materials
- None; foundational task

title: [0.2] Install + configure design tokens (rose/mauve palette + typography)
body:
## Goal
Drop the full design token system into `app/globals.css` so every component pulls from the same source of truth. Configure fonts via `next/font`.

## Context
Every subsequent issue depends on these tokens. Get this right and the system stays cohesive; get it wrong and we re-derive colors and font sizes across 30 components.

## Scope — in
- Modify `app/globals.css` to add CSS custom properties for:
  - Colors: `--bg`, `--bg-elev`, `--ink`, `--ink-dim`, `--ink-faint`, `--rule`, `--rule-bright`, `--accent`, `--accent-soft`, `--accent-deep`
  - Typography stacks: `--serif`, `--sans`, `--mono`
- Install fonts via `next/font/google` in `app/(v2)/layout.tsx`:
  - Instrument Serif (regular + italic)
  - Geist (300, 400, 500, 600)
  - JetBrains Mono (400, 500)
- Add the SVG noise overlay via `body::before` (data-URI)
- Add the ambient radial gradients on body background

## Scope — out
- Do NOT add components yet (issue #0.3)
- Do NOT touch the legacy `app/` routes' styling

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All tokens defined in `:root` block of `globals.css`
- [ ] Color values match the rose palette exactly:
  - `--accent: #d48aa6`
  - `--accent-soft: #e9bcce`
  - `--accent-deep: #8e5675`
  - `--bg: #0b0a0c`
- [ ] Fonts load with `display: 'swap'`
- [ ] No layout shift visible on `/v2` page load (font-display swap behaves correctly)
- [ ] Lighthouse mobile ≥ 90 on `/v2`

## Reference materials
- Mockup: `/mockups/homepage-v2-pink.html` (see `:root` CSS block at the top)
- All six mockup files have the same token block — copy from any

title: [0.3] Build shared layout primitives
body:
## Goal
Build the five reusable components every page depends on. Get these right and every page snaps together.

## Context
The mockups use the same visual primitives across all pages — section eyebrows with `§ NN` numbering, marginalia annotations, diamond markers, mono captions, section rules. Building them once means consistency for free.

## Scope — in
- Create `components/ui/eyebrow.tsx` — section eyebrow with `§` number, title, optional view-all link
- Create `components/ui/marginalia.tsx` — rotated annotation with arrow
- Create `components/ui/diamond-marker.tsx` — accent/standard/archive variants
- Create `components/ui/mono-caption.tsx` — small monospace caption wrapper
- Create `components/ui/section-rule.tsx` — horizontal divider with optional label
- All as Server Components (no "use client")
- All accept `className` for composition
- TypeScript: explicit prop types, no `any`

## Scope — out
- Do NOT build page-specific components (hero, work-row, etc. — those live in feature folders)
- Do NOT add any client-side interactivity

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All five components in `components/ui/`
- [ ] Each component has a TypeScript interface for props
- [ ] Each component renders correctly on a sandbox route `/v2/_sandbox` for visual verification
- [ ] No client JS shipped (verify via Network tab on `/v2/_sandbox`)
- [ ] All use design tokens, not hardcoded values

## Reference materials
- Mockups: any of them — same primitives across all six
- Visual inspection: `.eyebrow`, `.marginalia`, `.role::before` (diamond), `.section-eyebrow` blocks in CSS

title: [0.4] Set up MDX + content pipeline
body:
## Goal
Configure MDX so projects, essays, experience entries, and credentials can be authored as `.mdx` files with typed frontmatter.

## Context
Content is owned by frontmatter, not a CMS. This pipeline is the substrate for every page in epics 2–5.

## Scope — in
- Install `@next/mdx` (or `next-mdx-remote`, decide based on App Router stability)
- Configure `next.config.mjs` for `.mdx` route resolution
- Create folder structure:
  - `app/(v2)/_data/projects/*.mdx`
  - `app/(v2)/_data/essays/*.mdx`
  - `app/(v2)/_data/experience/*.mdx`
  - `app/(v2)/_data/credentials/*.mdx`
- Create typed loaders:
  - `lib/content/projects.ts` — `getAllProjects()`, `getProject(slug)`
  - `lib/content/essays.ts` — same pattern
  - Etc.
- Add Zod schemas (or similar) to validate frontmatter at build time
- Add one example MDX file per content type for verification

## Scope — out
- Do NOT write real content yet (separate writing issues in Epic 2–5)
- Do NOT build page rendering yet

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `getAllProjects()` returns typed array with sample data
- [ ] Invalid frontmatter throws at build time (test by intentionally breaking a schema)
- [ ] MDX bodies render as React (test with one `<Mdx />` component on sandbox)
- [ ] Frontmatter contract documented in a `README.md` inside `_data/`
- [ ] No TS errors

## Reference materials
- Frontmatter shapes described in the design conversation:
  - Project: `title, subtitle, slug, year, tier, order, dek, intro, metrics, stack, links, hasCaseStudy, heroArtifact`
  - Essay: `title, slug, publishedAt, dek, status, featured, tags`
  - Experience: `slug, title, company, start, end, summary, bullets, stack, metrics, tier`
  - Credential: `tier (degree/notable/archive), title, institution, dates, summary, relatedProjects`

title: [0.5] Set up CI guards (Lighthouse, type-check, Biome, Vercel previews)
body:
## Goal
Make the build self-policing. Every PR runs type-check, lint, build, and Lighthouse CI before merge.

## Context
Solo refactor + Claude Code authoring = the only thing standing between us and regressions is automation. Set it up once and never think about it again.

## Scope — in
- Add `.github/workflows/ci.yml` with:
  - TypeScript strict check (`tsc --noEmit`)
  - Biome / ESLint
  - `next build` with caching
  - Lighthouse CI on Vercel preview URL (or local build) with budget: mobile ≥ 90
- Enable Vercel preview deployments for `redesign` branch
- Set branch protection on `redesign`: require CI passing
- Set branch protection on `main`: require CI passing + 1 review

## Scope — out
- Do NOT add visual regression testing (overkill for solo)
- Do NOT add unit tests (separate concern; not part of this issue)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] CI workflow runs on every PR
- [ ] Failing TS triggers red X
- [ ] Failing Lighthouse triggers red X
- [ ] Vercel preview URL posts as a comment on every PR
- [ ] Cannot merge to `redesign` with red CI

## Reference materials
- Lighthouse CI: https://github.com/GoogleChrome/lighthouse-ci
- Next.js Vercel integration: enable in Vercel dashboard

title: [0.6] Audit + migrate content sources (backend API → MDX if applicable)
body:
## Goal
Decide where content lives in the new architecture, and migrate if needed. Most likely outcome: move from backend-served content to MDX-in-frontend.

## Context
Currently your portfolio may be fetching projects/skills/experience/articles from your backend repo's API. The new design treats content as code (MDX, version-controlled, build-time-rendered). This task audits the current state and migrates if applicable.

## Scope — in
- **Audit phase** — document where each content type currently lives:
  - Projects → backend API / static / DB?
  - Skills → backend API / static?
  - Experience → backend API / static?
  - Articles → backend API / external CMS?
  - Credentials → backend API / static?
- **Decision** — for each content type, decide: keep on backend, or migrate to MDX?
  - Default: migrate. Static content with low edit frequency belongs in the repo.
  - Exception: anything that genuinely needs real-time data or user-generated content stays on backend.
- **Migration** — for each migrated type:
  - Export current data to MDX files in `_data/<type>/*.mdx`
  - Match the frontmatter contracts from issue #0.4
  - Keep backend endpoints alive for now (cutover in Epic 6)

## Scope — out
- Do NOT delete backend endpoints yet (legacy site still uses them)
- Do NOT migrate writing content if you don't have any drafts yet — those issues are in Epic 3

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Audit document in `docs/content-audit.md` listing every content source
- [ ] Migration decision recorded per content type (keep / migrate)
- [ ] If migrating: existing content exported as MDX with correct frontmatter
- [ ] If keeping on backend: a typed fetch adapter in `lib/content/<type>.ts` that matches the same interface as the MDX loaders
- [ ] No data loss — every project/skill/experience that exists today is represented in the new system

## Reference materials
- Frontmatter contracts in issue #0.4
- Existing backend API documentation (in the docs repo)

title: [EPIC 1] Homepage
body:
## Epic goal
Ship the new homepage end-to-end at `/v2`. Highest recruiter-impact surface. After this epic, you can share the v2 URL with people and get feedback on a complete experience.

## Definition of done
- `/v2` route renders: hero + about + work preview + writing preview + contact + footer
- Single hero CTA, no gradient buttons, no floating WhatsApp
- All content sourced from MDX (or fetch adapters from #0.6)
- Lighthouse mobile ≥ 90
- Mobile responsive at 375px, 768px, 1280px

## Sub-issues
- [ ] [1.1] Implement `<Nav />` with simplified CTAs
- [ ] [1.2] Implement hero section
- [ ] [1.3] Implement about section
- [ ] [1.4] Implement work preview rows (3 projects)
- [ ] [1.5] Implement writing preview (1 featured + 3 recent)
- [ ] [1.6] Implement contact section
- [ ] [1.7] Implement footer with colophon
- [ ] [1.8] Remove old gradient CTA blocks + floating WhatsApp from legacy `/` route (cleanup)

title: [1.1] Implement <Nav /> with simplified CTAs
body:
## Goal
Build the sticky navigation bar with 6 text links + 1 mono Resume link. No outlined buttons, no Curriculum + Contacto duplication.

## Context
First visual element on every page. Sets the tone. Issue #1.2 (hero) depends on this layout.

## Scope — in
- Create `components/nav/site-nav.tsx` as a Server Component
- Render in `app/(v2)/layout.tsx`
- Sticky positioning with blur backdrop
- Active route highlighting via `usePathname` (this *one* part can be a tiny Client Component)
- Mobile: hide secondary links (Experience, Credentials) below 900px

## Scope — out
- Do NOT add mobile hamburger menu (defer to polish epic)
- Do NOT add command palette trigger here (separate issue in Epic 5)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Matches mockup `/mockups/homepage-v2-pink.html` `nav` block exactly
- [ ] Sticky with `backdrop-filter: blur(8px)`
- [ ] Active link highlighted in `--ink` color
- [ ] Resume link uses mono font, has left border, opens PDF in new tab
- [ ] Keyboard navigable; visible focus rings
- [ ] No layout shift on scroll

## Reference materials
- Mockup: `/mockups/homepage-v2-pink.html` → `<nav>` element

title: [1.2] Implement hero section
body:
## Goal
Replace the current homepage hero with the editorial hero: headline, dek, single CTA, no gradient buttons.

## Context
First visual a recruiter sees. Highest-impact change in Epic 1. Blocks #1.4 (work preview, which sits below this).

## Scope — in
- Modify `app/(v2)/page.tsx` (hero section only)
- Create `components/home/hero-headline.tsx`
- Create `components/home/availability-pill.tsx`
- Create `components/home/hero-cta.tsx`
- All Server Components

## Scope — out
- Do NOT touch about/work/writing sections (separate issues)
- Do NOT add the floating WhatsApp button (being removed)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Headline renders in Instrument Serif at `clamp(56px, 10vw, 160px)`
- [ ] Italic accent words use `--accent` token
- [ ] Single CTA "See selected work →" anchors to `#work`
- [ ] Resume link is a secondary text link in mono caption below CTA
- [ ] Availability pill has green pulsing dot (`#4ade80` — keep green, semantic)
- [ ] Hero sub-grid collapses to single column at < 1100px
- [ ] Lighthouse mobile ≥ 90 on `/v2`
- [ ] No client JS added — section is fully RSC

## Reference materials
- Mockup section: `/mockups/homepage-v2-pink.html` → `.hero` block

title: [1.3] Implement about section
body:
## Goal
Build the about section: portrait on the left, editorial text on the right, 6+ years typography stat, meta rows, single CTA.

## Context
Replaces the current "SOBRE MÍ" section with its gradient title and purple "6+ YEARS OF Experience" badge. The new treatment uses typography for the stat instead of a badge.

## Scope — in
- Create `components/home/about-section.tsx`
- Create `components/home/portrait.tsx` — accepts `src` prop for actual photo
- Use `next/image` with the photo cropped to 4:5 aspect
- `6+` stat: italic serif 64px in accent color
- Single CTA "Read the full track record →" links to `/v2/experience` (will exist after Epic 4)

## Scope — out
- Do NOT use a gradient frame on the portrait
- Do NOT use the purple "6+ YEARS OF Experience" badge
- Do NOT add social icons here — they belong in the contact section

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Portrait uses `next/image` with explicit `sizes` prop
- [ ] 4:5 aspect ratio enforced via `aspect-ratio` CSS
- [ ] Photo has 1px `--rule` border, no rounded corners > 2px
- [ ] About-stat block matches mockup typography
- [ ] Meta rows (based in / working / languages) in mono caption
- [ ] CTA link goes to `/v2/experience` (placeholder page OK if not built yet)
- [ ] Mobile: grid collapses to single column at < 1100px

## Reference materials
- Mockup: `/mockups/homepage-v2-pink.html` → `.about-grid` block

title: [1.4] Implement work preview rows (3 featured projects)
body:
## Goal
Build the 3-row work preview section that pulls from `_data/projects/*.mdx` filtered to `tier: 'hero' || tier: 'production'`.

## Context
This replaces the 2×2 bento grid. Each row is one-line title + dek + stack + metric + arrow, linking to `/v2/work/[slug]`. Depends on #0.4 (MDX pipeline) and #1.2 (hero — same anchor target).

## Scope — in
- Create `components/home/work-preview.tsx` (Server Component)
- Create `components/home/work-preview-row.tsx` (Server Component)
- Fetch projects via `getAllProjects()` from #0.4
- Filter and sort by `order` field
- Use `<Link prefetch>` to `/v2/work/[slug]` (route exists in Epic 2 — OK to link to 404 for now)
- Hover state: 8px manicule slide-in on left, accent-soft on title, arrow translateX

## Scope — out
- Do NOT build the `/v2/work/[slug]` detail pages (Epic 2)
- Do NOT build the `/v2/work` index (Epic 2)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Renders exactly 3 rows (hero + first 2 production)
- [ ] Each row links to `/v2/work/[slug]` with prefetch
- [ ] Hover manicule animation is CSS-only (no JS)
- [ ] Stack and metric column hidden below 1100px (mockup behavior)
- [ ] Footer link "Read all case studies →" goes to `/v2/work`
- [ ] Grid pulls from MDX, not hardcoded — verify by adding a 4th project and confirming it doesn't render

## Reference materials
- Mockup: `/mockups/homepage-v2-pink.html` → `.work-preview-grid` block
- Frontmatter contract: see #0.4 acceptance criteria

title: [1.5] Implement writing preview (1 featured + 3 recent)
body:
## Goal
Build the asymmetric 2/3 + 1/3 writing preview: featured essay on the left, three recent essays in compact format on the right.

## Context
Demonstrates you write seriously *and* often, in 4 seconds of scan time. Depends on #0.4 and the essays MDX folder.

## Scope — in
- Create `components/home/writing-preview.tsx`
- Create `components/home/featured-essay-card.tsx`
- Create `components/home/essay-list-item.tsx`
- Fetch via `getAllEssays()`, select featured (`featured: true`) + 3 most recent
- Each item links to `/v2/writing/[slug]`

## Scope — out
- Do NOT build `/v2/writing` archive (Epic 3)
- Do NOT build `/v2/writing/[slug]` detail pages (Epic 3)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Featured essay rendered with 48px italic serif title
- [ ] Three recent essays in compact list with date + title
- [ ] Border-right divider between columns; collapses to stacked at < 1100px
- [ ] All links use `<Link prefetch>`
- [ ] If no essays exist in MDX, render a graceful empty state (small mono caption "— first essay coming soon")

## Reference materials
- Mockup: `/mockups/homepage-v2-pink.html` → `.writing-preview` block

title: [1.6] Implement contact section
body:
## Goal
Replace the two gradient CTA blocks (¿Te gustó lo que viste? + Hablemos calendar) with a single editorial contact section.

## Context
This is the conversion endpoint. Single primary CTA (Book a 30-min call) weighted as italic serif; secondary contacts as mono text links on the same line. Cultural caveat: WhatsApp stays as a contact option with `(LATAM)` annotation.

## Scope — in
- Create `components/home/contact-section.tsx`
- Italic serif headline + sans dek + horizontal links row
- Contact links: Book a 30-min call (primary) · email · linkedin · github · whatsapp (LATAM)
- Bottom microcopy: time zone, response time, no-recruiter-spam line
- Real calendar booking link in env var `NEXT_PUBLIC_CALENDAR_URL`

## Scope — out
- Do NOT add a contact form (use external calendar / email)
- Do NOT add a floating WhatsApp button anywhere
- Do NOT use any pink-cyan-purple gradient

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Single primary CTA, 4 secondary text links
- [ ] No buttons, no gradient backgrounds
- [ ] Booking link from env var (with safe fallback to `mailto:`)
- [ ] Mobile: links stack vertically with proper gap
- [ ] Vertical accent line above section (`.contact::before` in mockup)

## Reference materials
- Mockup: `/mockups/homepage-v2-pink.html` → `.contact` section

title: [1.7] Implement footer with colophon
body:
## Goal
Build the typographic footer with build credits, last-updated date, GitHub source link, and secondary nav.

## Context
Senior engineers read colophons. Linking the GitHub source of the portfolio is the ultimate flex — dares the recruiter to evaluate your code.

## Scope — in
- Create `components/site/footer.tsx`
- Render in `app/(v2)/layout.tsx` (below main content)
- Two columns: colophon (left), secondary nav (right)
- `Last updated` date pulled from build-time env var or git commit timestamp

## Scope — out
- Do NOT add social icons here — they live in contact section
- Do NOT add newsletter signup (separate concern)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Colophon shows: name, build stack (Next.js 15 · Instrument Serif & Geist · Vercel), last updated, GitHub source link
- [ ] Secondary nav: Work · Writing · About · Contact · RSS
- [ ] All in mono caption font
- [ ] Mobile: stacks to single column at < 900px
- [ ] "Last updated" reflects actual build date (env var or git)

## Reference materials
- Mockup: `/mockups/homepage-v2-pink.html` → `<footer>`

title: [1.8] Remove old gradient CTA blocks + floating WhatsApp from legacy route
body:
## Goal
Once `/v2` homepage is feature-complete, clean up the legacy `/` homepage to remove the gradient CTA blocks and floating WhatsApp.

## Context
NOT a cutover — the legacy `/` still works as the live site until Epic 6. But removing the WhatsApp bubble and gradient blocks is a low-risk cleanup that improves the live site immediately.

## Scope — in
- Remove `Hablemos` CTA gradient block from legacy homepage
- Remove `Agendar una llamada` gradient block from legacy homepage
- Remove floating WhatsApp button component
- Replace with a single quiet "Contact" link routed to `mailto:` (or existing contact page) — minimum viable, just to keep the conversion path alive

## Scope — out
- Do NOT redesign the legacy homepage (it's getting retired in Epic 6 anyway)
- Do NOT remove other CTAs yet (those are in Epic 6 cutover)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Live `/` no longer shows the two gradient CTA blocks
- [ ] Floating WhatsApp button removed from all pages
- [ ] Single "Contact" link still present (no dead-end UX)
- [ ] Site builds and deploys without errors

title: [EPIC 2] Selected Work
body:
## Epic goal
Build `/v2/work` index page + `/v2/work/[slug]` case study detail pages. Write three real case studies. This is the conversion page.

## Definition of done
- `/v2/work` renders hero + 2 production + archive index
- Three case study detail pages exist with 1200–1800 words each
- Inline SVG diagram for hero project (ACO-Firefly)
- Mock screenshots replaced with real `next/image` assets where possible
- All linked from homepage via Epic 1

## Sub-issues
- [ ] [2.1] Implement `/v2/work` page shell
- [ ] [2.2] Implement hero case study layout
- [ ] [2.3] Build inline SVG diagram for hero project (ACO-Firefly)
- [ ] [2.4] Implement production-row layout (split-row, alternating)
- [ ] [2.5] Implement screenshot components (real or styled mocks)
- [ ] [2.6] Implement archive index rows
- [ ] [2.7] Write case study #1 (ACO-Firefly hero) — `needs-writing`
- [ ] [2.8] Write case study #2 (Fin) — `needs-writing`
- [ ] [2.9] Write case study #3 (Auto Parts ID) — `needs-writing`
- [ ] [2.10] Implement `/v2/work/[slug]` case study detail page layout

title: [2.1] Implement /v2/work page shell
body:
## Goal
Build the top-level `/v2/work` index page shell — eyebrow, headline, preamble, footer note.

## Scope — in
- Create `app/(v2)/work/page.tsx` (Server Component)
- Eyebrow: § 02 · Selected Work
- Headline: "Twelve projects. Three worth defending."
- Preamble: italic serif sub-statement
- Footer note: editorial italic serif paragraph

## Scope — out
- Do NOT build hero case study layout (issue #2.2)
- Do NOT build rows yet

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Matches mockup `/mockups/work-mockup.html` top + footer sections
- [ ] All typography uses tokens
- [ ] Page renders even when `_data/projects` is empty (graceful fallback)

## Reference materials
- Mockup: `/mockups/work-mockup.html` → `.eyebrow` + `.title-row` + `.work-footnote`

title: [2.2] Implement hero case study layout
body:
## Goal
Build the two-column hero case study block (text + diagram column) for the single `tier: 'hero'` project.

## Scope — in
- Create `components/work/hero-case-study.tsx`
- Render on `/v2/work` page below shell
- Two-column grid: text left, diagram (or hero image) right
- Text column: title, subtitle, context strip, intro paragraphs, 3 metrics, stack, links row
- Marginalia ("the piece I'd lead an interview with") rotated annotation

## Scope — out
- Do NOT build the SVG diagram (#2.3)
- Do NOT build case-study detail page rendering (#2.10)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Enforces exactly one `tier: 'hero'` project (throws at build if not)
- [ ] Three metrics use italic serif with accent color
- [ ] Marginalia hidden below 1100px
- [ ] Two-column collapses at < 1100px

## Reference materials
- Mockup: `/mockups/work-mockup.html` → `.hero-case` block

title: [2.3] Build inline SVG diagram for hero project (ACO-Firefly)
body:
## Goal
Build the inline SVG architecture diagram that replaces the broken-image placeholder. Shows hierarchical structure: Layer 01 (ACO ants with pheromone trails) → Layer 02 (firefly clusters) → final signature output.

## Context
This is the killer differentiator — proves you can do novel work, not just card layouts. Rendered server-side as inline SVG: zero JS, scales to any device, ships effectively zero bytes beyond the markup.

## Scope — in
- Create `components/work/diagrams/aco-firefly-diagram.tsx` (Server Component)
- Inline SVG, no external assets
- All colors from design tokens
- Caption below: small monospace explanation

## Scope — out
- Do NOT use a library like d3 (overkill, ships JS)
- Do NOT use rendered images

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Renders identically across all browsers and device sizes (`viewBox` based)
- [ ] Uses only `--ink-dim`, `--accent`, `--accent-soft` for colors
- [ ] Zero JS shipped
- [ ] Matches mockup `/mockups/work-mockup.html` `.hero-diagram svg` exactly (copy-paste OK, then refactor into JSX)

## Reference materials
- Mockup: `/mockups/work-mockup.html` → SVG block inside `.hero-diagram`

title: [2.4] Implement production-row layout (split-row, alternating)
body:
## Goal
Build the alternating 60/40 split-row layout for the 2 production projects (Fin, Auto Parts ID).

## Scope — in

- Text column: number tag, italic serif title, italic serif dek, body paragraph, metric row, stack, links
- Image column: `next/image` or mock screenshot component (issue #2.5)
- Renders all `tier: 'production'` projects

## Scope — out
- Do NOT zig-zag between far left and far right (mockup uses `.flipped` for column order only, content stays full-width)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Two rows rendered (Fin, Auto Parts ID — both `tier: 'production'`)
- [ ] Alternating layout (first standard, second flipped)
- [ ] Metric row in mono with accent-color values in italic serif
- [ ] Mobile: collapses to stacked single column

## Reference materials
- Mockup: `/mockups/work-mockup.html` → `.prod-row` and `.prod-row.flipped`

title: [2.5] Implement screenshot components (real or styled mocks)
body:
## Goal
Build the screenshot components — either real `next/image` for projects with screenshots, or styled mock components for projects without.

## Context
Fin and Auto Parts ID don't have great screenshots yet. The mockup uses styled inline HTML components that demonstrate the product (USD→ARS conversion comparison, parts catalog grid). These are honest placeholders that *demonstrate the work*.

## Scope — in

- Create `components/work/screenshots/fin-currency-mock.tsx` — IEEE 754 vs Fin engine comparison
- Create `components/work/screenshots/parts-catalog-mock.tsx` — brake-system catalog grid
- All Server Components, no JS

## Scope — out
- Do NOT use the broken-image placeholder anywhere
- Do NOT use stock photos

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All components render at 16:10 aspect ratio
- [ ] All use design tokens for colors
- [ ] Browser frame has 3 dots + mock URL in mono
- [ ] When you have real screenshots, swap-in is a single prop change

## Reference materials

title: [2.7] Write case study #1 — ACO-Firefly hero project
body:
## Goal
Write the full case study for the hero project. 1200–1800 words of long-form prose, MDX-authored.

## Context
This is the conversion-critical piece of writing on the entire portfolio. It must read like a senior engineer's working notebook, not an academic abstract. **This is a writing task you do yourself** — do not hand to Claude Code.

## Scope — in
- Create `app/(v2)/_data/projects/aco-firefly-genomic.mdx`
- Frontmatter per #0.4 contract
- Body structure:
  1. The problem in human English (high-dim oncology data, instability)
  2. Why standard methods fail
  3. The hybridization idea + why hierarchical
  4. Implementation choices + tradeoffs
  5. Results + what generalizes
  6. What I'd do differently
- Embed the SVG diagram component (#2.3) inline

## Scope — out
- Do NOT use "is designed as," "according to keywords," or other LLM-template phrases
- Do NOT bullet-point the whole essay; prose with restraint

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] 1200–1800 words
- [ ] Written in first person, present-leaning past tense
- [ ] At least 2 embedded code or formula snippets
- [ ] Inline diagram embedded
- [ ] No AI-template-tells (run it past a senior friend if uncertain)
- [ ] Spell-checked and proofread

title: [2.8] Write case study #2 — Fin currency engine
body:
## Goal
Write the full case study for Fin. 1200–1800 words. Focus on the IEEE 754 decision and why decimal arithmetic was non-negotiable for personal finance.

## Scope — in
- Create `app/(v2)/_data/projects/fin-currency-engine.mdx`
- Body structure:
  1. The bug that motivated the project (a real bug you observed)
  2. Why IEEE 754 was the wrong primitive
  3. The decimal-arithmetic decision + library choices
  4. Architecture: how rates, snapshots, conversions compose
  5. What it took to ship to ~600 daily users
  6. What I'd change

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] 1200–1800 words
- [ ] At least one code snippet showing IEEE 754 bug
- [ ] At least one code snippet showing the fix
- [ ] No template phrases
- [ ] Honest about scope and limitations

title: [2.9] Write case study #3 — Auto Parts ID
body:
## Goal
Write the full case study for Auto Parts ID. 1200–1800 words. Focus on the parts-fitment graph problem and the sole-founding-engineer reality.

## Scope — in
- Create `app/(v2)/_data/projects/auto-parts-id.mdx`
- Body structure:
  1. The business context (B2B automotive distribution)
  2. Why headless on Medusa.js v2 over alternatives
  3. The parts-fitment graph problem (TecDoc, ACES/PIES)
  4. Data pipeline architecture
  5. What it's like being sole eng on a project this size
  6. What's still ahead

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] 1200–1800 words
- [ ] At least one data-schema or pipeline diagram
- [ ] Honest about what's not yet shipped
- [ ] No template phrases

title: [2.10] Implement /v2/work/[slug] case study detail page
body:
## Goal
Build the long-form case study detail page that renders MDX content for each project with `hasCaseStudy: true`.

## Scope — in
- Create `app/(v2)/work/[slug]/page.tsx` with `generateStaticParams`
- Long-form reading layout: max-width ~680px for body, generous leading
- Drop-cap on first paragraph
- `generateMetadata` for per-case-study OG tags
- Embed the case-study hero (title + dek + metrics) at top
- MDX body rendered with custom components for code blocks, diagrams, images
- "Related projects" footer (other projects excluding current)

## Scope — out
- Do NOT add comments or share buttons
- Do NOT add a sidebar TOC (defer to polish)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Static generation for all case studies at build
- [ ] Body type renders at 17–18px with 1.7 line-height
- [ ] Code blocks have syntax highlighting (Shiki preferred — zero-runtime)
- [ ] Drop-cap on first paragraph using `::first-letter`
- [ ] 404 for invalid slugs
- [ ] OG image generated per slug (placeholder OK for now — Epic 6 polishes)

## Reference materials
- Mockup: not yet built — reuse long-form layout pattern from writing-mockup.html if needed
- MDX rendering: `next-mdx-remote` or `@next/mdx` per #0.4

title: [3.6] Write 2 essays for initial publication
body:
## Goal
Publish 2 essays so the writing section isn't empty at launch. Topics you've already lived through — don't invent new ones.

## Context
Both essays exist as MDX with frontmatter from #0.4. One should be `featured: true` (likely "Why test-coverage metrics are lying" or the IEEE 754 piece). **Writing task** — do not hand to Claude Code.

## Scope — in
- Create 2 MDX files in `_data/essays/`
- Suggested topics (pick what you've actually done):
  1. Avoiding floating-point pitfalls in financial applications (if Fin is shipped)
  2. A year of building with LLMs in production (if Anthropic/Revelo work is publishable)
  3. Why test-coverage metrics are lying to your team
- Each 800–1500 words

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Both files have valid frontmatter
- [ ] Exactly one has `featured: true`
- [ ] No AI-template phrases
- [ ] First-person voice, present-leaning past tense

title: [3.7] Set up RSS route handler
body:
## Goal
Implement `/writing/feed.xml` as a Next.js Route Handler returning a valid Atom/RSS 2.0 feed.

## Context
RSS is the engineering-manager signal — they use Feedly/NetNewsWire and will subscribe if a feed exists.

## Scope — in
- Create `app/(v2)/writing/feed.xml/route.ts`
- Generate from `getAllEssays()` filtered to `status: 'published'`
- Cache headers: 1 hour revalidation
- Include title, dek, link, pubDate per item

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Validates with https://validator.w3.org/feed
- [ ] Returns `application/atom+xml` or `application/rss+xml` content type
- [ ] Includes site-wide metadata + per-item entries

title: [EPIC 4] Experience page
body:
## Epic goal
Build `/v2/experience` with the depth-dial CV (glance / highlights / full modes), metric typography, archive disclosure for pre-2022 roles.

## Definition of done
- All 12+ experience entries migrated to typed data with tier classification
- Three modes work via `?view=` URL param
- Numbers in bullets typographically emphasized
- Pre-2022 roles collapsed by default

## Sub-issues
- [ ] [4.1] Migrate experience data to typed MDX with tier classification
- [ ] [4.2] Implement `/v2/experience` page layout — left-rail CV with year column
- [ ] [4.3] Implement depth-dial mode toggle with `nuqs` (glance/highlights/full)
- [ ] [4.4] Implement metric-emphasis typography pass (server-side)
- [ ] [4.5] Implement archive disclosure for pre-2022 roles

title: [4.1] Migrate experience data to typed MDX with tier classification
body:
## Goal
Convert all 12+ experience entries into MDX files with frontmatter per #0.4 contract. Manually classify each as `tier: 'flagship' | 'standard' | 'archive'`.

## Scope — in
- Create `_data/experience/*.mdx` for every role
- Frontmatter: `slug, title, company, start, end, summary, bullets, stack, metrics, tier`
- Extract numerical metrics (300ms, 99.9%, 30%, etc.) into structured `metrics` array
- Tier classification:
  - `flagship` (always expanded in highlights): 3–4 roles, most recent + most relevant
  - `standard`: visible but collapsed by default in highlights
  - `archive`: pre-2022, behind disclosure

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every current experience entry has an MDX file
- [ ] Exactly 3–4 marked `tier: 'flagship'`
- [ ] All `archive` entries are pre-2022
- [ ] Metrics extracted into structured fields (not just in body text)
- [ ] Frontmatter validates at build

title: [4.2] Implement /v2/experience page layout — left-rail CV
body:
## Goal
Build the single-column left-rail CV layout (no zig-zag). Year column on left, role content on right, diamond markers at title level.

## Scope — in
- Create `app/(v2)/experience/page.tsx`
- Create `components/experience/role-entry.tsx`
- Vertical hairline rule at 140px from left
- Diamond marker color by tier (accent / dim / outline)
- Title in italic serif, company on same line after em-dash

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Matches mockup `/mockups/experience-mockup.html` `.cv` block
- [ ] No alternating left/right layout
- [ ] Markers correctly colored by tier
- [ ] Mobile: rule moves to 12px, year column shrinks to 80px

title: [4.3] Implement depth-dial mode toggle with nuqs
body:
## Goal
Add the `?view=glance|highlights|full` URL-state toggle and the server-side rendering logic that shows/hides content per mode.

## Scope — in
- Install `nuqs` if not already
- Create `components/experience/view-toggle.tsx` (Client Component, uses `useQueryState` with `shallow: false`)
- Modify page Server Component to read `searchParams.view` and conditionally render bullets/summary
- Glance: title line only with one inlined metric
- Highlights (default): flagship roles expand fully, standard show summary only
- Full: everything expanded

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] URL updates as `?view=glance` / `?view=highlights` / `?view=full`
- [ ] Server re-renders for each mode (verify via Network tab)
- [ ] "Recommended" badge on Highlights mode
- [ ] Crossfade transition between modes (140ms)

## Reference materials
- Mockup: `/mockups/experience-mockup.html` → `.mode-toggle` and JS at the bottom
- `nuqs` docs: https://nuqs.47ng.com

title: [4.4] Implement metric-emphasis typography (server-side)
body:
## Goal
Make every numerical metric in bullet text typographically magnetic — italic serif at 1.2em in accent color.

## Context
This is the single highest-ROI typographic move. Recruiters' eyes lock onto numbers; make them lockable.

## Scope — in
- Create `lib/typography/interpolate-metrics.tsx` — server-side function that takes `text` + `metrics[]` and returns JSX with metric spans wrapped
- Use it inside `<BulletText>` component
- Add `.metric` CSS class to `globals.css` with the emphasis styling

## Scope — out
- Do NOT hardcode `<strong>` in MDX content
- Do NOT use regex parsing of body text — use the structured metrics array

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All bullet text with metrics renders with emphasized numbers
- [ ] One global CSS rule controls all metric appearance (verify by changing it once and seeing all numbers update)
- [ ] No client JS

title: [4.5] Implement archive disclosure for pre-2022 roles
body:
## Goal
Hide pre-2022 roles behind a single `<details>`-style disclosure with "— Pre-2022 · N earlier roles · click to expand" label.

## Scope — in
- Create `components/experience/archive-disclosure.tsx`
- Use native `<details>`/`<summary>` with CSS-only expand animation
- `interpolate-size: allow-keywords` + `transition-behavior: allow-discrete` for the modern animation
- Dashed border styling to read as "footnote, not body" 

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Works without JS (native `<details>` fallback)
- [ ] Smooth expand animation when JS available
- [ ] Marker icon flips from + to - on expand

title: [EPIC 6] Polish + cutover
body:
## Epic goal
Final polish: view transitions, per-page OG images, bilingual content, a11y pass, performance pass. Then retire `/v2` prefix and cutover to production.

## Definition of done
- All `/v2/*` routes promoted to `/` root
- Legacy routes redirect to new equivalents
- Lighthouse mobile ≥ 95 on every page
- Bilingual EN/ES working
- WCAG AA passing

## Sub-issues
- [ ] [6.1] Implement view transitions API for inter-page navigation
- [ ] [6.2] Generate per-page OG images with `@vercel/og`
- [ ] [6.3] Implement bilingual EN/ES content with locale-aware archive surfacing
- [ ] [6.4] Performance pass — verify Lighthouse ≥ 95 mobile everywhere
- [ ] [6.5] A11y pass — keyboard nav, focus rings, prefers-reduced-motion, screen-reader testing
- [ ] [6.6] Cut over from `/v2/*` to root, redirect legacy URLs
- [ ] [6.7] Update social profiles (LinkedIn, GitHub bio, Twitter) with new portfolio URL/messaging
- [ ] [6.8] Write a launch announcement essay (optional but recommended) — `needs-writing`

title: [6.1] Implement view transitions API
body:
## Goal
Enable Next.js 15's View Transitions for smooth inter-page navigation, especially homepage → case study handoffs.

## Scope — in
- Enable `experimental.viewTransition: true` in `next.config.mjs`
- Add `viewTransitionName` to hero images and project titles
- Define CSS animations for the transitions
- Gate behind `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)`

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Hero image animates from work card to case study page
- [ ] Reduced-motion users get instant transitions
- [ ] No layout shift during transition

title: [6.2] Generate per-page OG images with @vercel/og
body:
## Goal
Auto-generate Open Graph images per case study and per essay, matching the portfolio's typography.

## Scope — in
- Install `@vercel/og`
- Create `app/api/og/route.tsx` Edge route
- Generate from title + dek + author
- Use Instrument Serif via font import (`fetch`'d at build)
- Wire up `generateMetadata` in case-study and essay pages

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Each case study has unique OG image at `/api/og?slug=X`
- [ ] Each essay has unique OG image
- [ ] Twitter/LinkedIn preview shows the generated image
- [ ] Image size 1200×630, < 100KB

title: [6.3] Implement bilingual EN/ES content
body:
## Goal
Surface bilingual content correctly: per-locale MDX files where they exist, "Available in EN/ES only" caption where they don't.

## Scope — in
- Use `next-intl` or similar for `/[locale]/...` routing
- MDX naming convention: `slug.en.mdx` / `slug.es.mdx` OR single file with `translations` frontmatter
- Locale-aware archive: show all entries, mark untranslated with "↗ ES only" caption

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Both `/en/work` and `/es/work` render
- [ ] Untranslated content surfaced (not hidden) with locale flag
- [ ] Language toggle preserves current route + query state

title: [6.4] Performance pass — Lighthouse ≥ 95 mobile everywhere
body:
## Goal
Audit every page with Lighthouse mobile and resolve any score < 95.

## Scope — in
- Run Lighthouse on every route
- Resolve LCP, CLS, FCP issues
- Verify `next/image` `sizes` props are correct
- Verify `next/font` is using `display: 'swap'` and `preload`
- Bundle-analyze; tree-shake anything unexpected

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Every public route scores ≥ 95 mobile on Lighthouse
- [ ] No CLS > 0.05 anywhere
- [ ] LCP < 2.5s on slow 4G

title: [6.5] A11y pass
body:
## Goal
WCAG AA compliance on every page.

## Scope — in
- Keyboard navigate every page; verify focus rings visible everywhere
- Run axe-core; resolve violations
- Test with VoiceOver or NVDA on at least homepage + work + writing
- Verify `prefers-reduced-motion` gates all animations
- Verify color contrast: `--ink-dim` on `--bg` ≥ 4.5:1

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] axe-core: zero violations on all routes
- [ ] All interactive elements keyboard-reachable
- [ ] Focus rings visible (not removed by `outline: none` without replacement)
- [ ] Screen reader announces sections correctly

title: [6.6] Cutover from /v2/* to root
body:
## Goal
Promote the new design to production. Move `app/(v2)/*` to `app/*` (or remove the route group prefix), delete the old route files, add redirects for old URLs.

## Context
This is the irreversible-ish step. Do it after Epic 6 polish is complete and you've shared the v2 URL with 2–3 trusted reviewers.

## Scope — in
- Move all files from `app/(v2)/` to `app/` (or rename group)
- Delete legacy route files
- Add `next.config.mjs` redirects for any legacy URLs that need to keep working
- Update sitemap.xml + robots.txt
- Merge `redesign` branch to `main`

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Production URL serves the new design
- [ ] No 404s for previously-indexed URLs (check Google Search Console)
- [ ] Sitemap reflects new structure

title: [6.7] Update social profiles with new portfolio URL/messaging
body:
## Goal
After cutover, refresh LinkedIn headline, GitHub bio, Twitter/X bio with new portfolio messaging and stack focus.

## Scope — in
- LinkedIn: new headline, About section reflecting AI-eng + senior frontend positioning
- GitHub: pinned repos, updated bio
- Twitter/X: bio update, portfolio link
- Any other developer-presence surfaces (dev.to, Mastodon, Bluesky)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All public profiles link to new portfolio
- [ ] Messaging aligned (no profile says "Fullstack Developer" while portfolio says "AI-eng & senior frontend")

title: [6.8] Write launch announcement essay
body:
## Goal
Optional but recommended: write a short essay-cum-changelog explaining what changed about the portfolio and why. Becomes the first "featured" essay at launch.

## Context
Recruiters who knew the old site appreciate the meta-narrative. Future you appreciates having the rationale documented. **Writing task.**

## Scope — in
- ~800 words MDX essay
- Topics: why the redesign, the editorial principles, the anti-AI design manifesto
- Mark as `featured: true` (replaces previous featured essay)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Published, `featured: true`
- [ ] Reflects the actual reasoning behind decisions
